meta_title: Impact Doors in Palm Beach Gardens, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Palm Beach Gardens, FL. Protect your home in PGA National, Mirasol & beyond. Free quotes, financing from $87/mo.
h1: Impact Doors in Palm Beach Gardens, FL — Built for Beauty, Engineered for the Storm
intro:
Palm Beach Gardens is one of South Florida's most coveted addresses — a master-planned city where championship golf courses border upscale communities like PGA National, Mirasol, Old Palm, and Frenchman's Reserve. With an average home value hovering around $580,000, residents here have invested significantly in their properties, their lifestyles, and their futures. Protecting that investment starts at the threshold — literally — with impact-resistant doors designed to withstand the forces that South Florida storms routinely deliver. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we bring that protection directly to your door, with zero shortcuts and zero subcontractors.

Despite sitting approximately five miles from the Atlantic coastline, Palm Beach Gardens is far from sheltered when it comes to hurricane exposure. The city sits in a 170 mph wind zone under Florida Building Code classifications, meaning every exterior door on your home must be rated to resist extraordinary pressure, wind-borne debris, and prolonged storm conditions. Older homes — roughly 30% of the housing stock was built before 1994's landmark code revisions — may still have original entry doors, sliding glass doors, or French doors that simply don't meet today's standards. Upgrading to certified impact doors isn't just a smart home improvement; in many cases, it's a code compliance necessity when you sell, remodel, or re-roof your home.

From grand estate entries along Old Palm Golf Club to the resort-style rear lanais common in Evergrene and Frenchman's Reserve, Palm Beach Gardens homes demand impact doors that balance security and aesthetics without compromise. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI impact door systems — premium brands trusted across Palm Beach County — featuring multi-point locking mechanisms, laminated impact glass, and frames engineered for Florida's heat, humidity, and hurricane seasons. Whether you're replacing a single front entry door or upgrading every opening in your home, our 60+ years of combined installation experience ensures the job is done right the first time.

section_why.h2: Why Palm Beach Gardens Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
When you live in a community like PGA National or Mirasol, you expect the contractors who work on your home to match the standards of the neighborhood — professional, detail-oriented, and accountable from start to finish. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation as 'The Kings of Service' on exactly that standard. We are a fully licensed and insured company with an A+ BBB rating and more than 500 completed homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. Every single installation we perform is handled by our own in-house team — never a subcontractor, never a day laborer, always your dedicated Premier Impact Windows & Roofing crew.

That 100% in-house installation model matters more than most homeowners realize. When an installation team works exclusively for one company, they are trained to that company's standards, accountable to that company's reputation, and familiar with the nuances of the products they install. Our technicians have seen every door configuration Palm Beach Gardens homes present — from the sweeping double-entry doors in Old Palm Golf Club estates to the multi-panel sliding glass systems that open onto screened enclosures in Evergrene. With 60+ years of combined experience among our crew, there is no learning curve on your property.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing also handles every step of the process, including pulling the required permits from the City of Palm Beach Gardens Building Division, coordinating inspections, and ensuring your installation passes final approval without delays. Many homeowners in the ZIP codes 33418, 33410, and 33412 have been surprised to learn that an unlicensed or improperly permitted door installation can void their homeowner's insurance coverage — a costly mistake that our process is specifically designed to prevent. We manage the paperwork so you can focus on enjoying your home.

Finally, we offer accessible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, making it possible for Palm Beach Gardens homeowners at every stage of homeownership to upgrade their doors without waiting for the next storm to make the decision for them. Quality impact door protection should not be a luxury reserved only for those who can pay in full upfront — and with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, it isn't.

section_history.h2: Palm Beach Gardens Has a Hurricane History Worth Taking Seriously
section_history.body:
Palm Beach Gardens residents who moved here after 2005 may have a false sense of calm when it comes to hurricane risk. But the city's storm record since 1990 tells a different story — nine named storms have impacted the area, including three devastating blows in back-to-back years that permanently reshaped how South Florida approaches home protection. In 2004, Hurricane Frances made landfall near Stuart and tracked directly over Palm Beach County, battering Gardens communities with sustained winds and extended rain bands for over 24 hours. Just three weeks later, Hurricane Jeanne followed a nearly identical path, striking homes that were already weakened and whose entry doors, sliding glass doors, and garage doors had already been tested to their limits. The combination of Frances and Jeanne was catastrophic for insurance claims across the county, with thousands of homes suffering interior water damage after exterior doors failed.

In 2005, Hurricane Wilma — a Category 3 storm at landfall — raced across South Florida from the southwest and caught many Palm Beach Gardens homeowners off guard after years of storms tracking from the east. The fast-moving nature of Wilma generated powerful pressure differentials that caused improperly installed or non-impact-rated doors to bow inward or fail entirely, allowing wind and water to enter homes in communities from PGA National to the eastern corridors near Juno Beach. Then, in 2017, Hurricane Irma reminded a new generation of Palm Beach Gardens residents that no season is truly safe, bringing tropical-storm-to-Category-1 conditions to the area and once again exposing the vulnerability of older doors and unsealed openings. Each of these storms underscored the same lesson: when it comes to your entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors, impact-rated products are not optional — they are essential.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Palm Beach Gardens
section_code.body:
Palm Beach Gardens falls under the jurisdiction of the Florida Building Code (FBC), 8th Edition, and while the city is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies specifically to Miami-Dade and Broward counties — it is absolutely not exempt from stringent hurricane protection requirements. The city sits in a 170 mph wind speed design zone, which means all exterior doors, including entry doors, French doors, and sliding glass doors, must be tested and certified to withstand wind loads consistent with that exposure. Products used must carry appropriate Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or Florida Product Approval documentation, even outside of the HVHZ boundary.

All impact door installations in Palm Beach Gardens require a permit pulled through the City of Palm Beach Gardens Building Division, located at City Hall on Burns Road. Permitting requirements include product approval documentation, signed and sealed drawings from a licensed engineer or architect for certain configurations, and a final inspection by a city building official upon completion. Homeowners who skip the permit process — sometimes tempted by contractors offering cash discounts to avoid paperwork — risk fines, insurance claim denials, and complications at the time of home sale. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process as a standard part of every project, ensuring your installation is code-compliant and fully documented.

For homes built before 1994, when Florida's post-Hurricane Andrew building codes were introduced, the gap between existing door specifications and current code requirements can be significant. Approximately 30% of Palm Beach Gardens homes fall into this pre-1994 category, and many still feature original hollow-core entry doors or aluminum sliding glass doors with single-pane glass that would not survive a modern hurricane impact test. Replacing these with FBC-compliant, impact-rated door systems from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings your home into full compliance and eliminates one of the most common points of catastrophic failure during a storm.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Products for Palm Beach Gardens Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading impact door brands — ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI — selected specifically for their performance credentials, aesthetic versatility, and long-term durability in South Florida's demanding climate. Both product lines are tested and certified to meet Florida Building Code requirements for Palm Beach County's 170 mph wind zone, and both carry the product approval documentation required by the City of Palm Beach Gardens Building Division for permitted installations.

ESW's impact door line is a favorite among Palm Beach Gardens homeowners who want the highest level of storm protection without sacrificing curb appeal. Available in a wide range of entry door configurations — from single and double panel doors to oversized estate-style entries — ESW doors feature laminated impact glass, heavy-duty multi-point locking systems, and thermally broken aluminum or reinforced fiberglass frames that resist both wind pressure and the relentless South Florida heat. The multi-point locking systems are a particularly important feature: unlike traditional single-latch doors, multi-point locks engage at the top, bottom, and sides of the door frame simultaneously, creating a seal that dramatically increases resistance to both forced entry and wind-driven pressure during a storm. This makes ESW doors an ideal choice for the high-value estates and luxury communities that define much of Palm Beach Gardens' residential landscape.

CWI impact doors bring similar performance standards with a product range optimized for sliding glass door and French door applications — the configurations most commonly found along the pool decks, patios, and rear lanais of communities like Frenchman's Reserve, Evergrene, and PGA National. CWI's sliding glass door systems use heavy-gauge extruded aluminum frames, interlocking meeting rails, and laminated impact glass to create a barrier that is virtually indistinguishable from a standard sliding door in appearance but radically different in storm performance. For homeowners who entertain frequently or value the indoor-outdoor flow that defines the South Florida lifestyle, CWI's systems offer the rare combination of effortless daily usability and uncompromising hurricane protection.

section_cost.h2: What Do Impact Doors Cost in Palm Beach Gardens — and What's the Return?
section_cost.body:
In a city where the average home value sits at $580,000, impact door upgrades represent one of the highest-ROI home improvements available to Palm Beach Gardens homeowners. The upfront cost of impact door installation varies depending on door type, size, configuration, and the number of openings being replaced. A single impact-rated entry door installation typically ranges from $2,500 to $5,500, while larger French door or sliding glass door systems — common in the resort-style homes throughout communities like Mirasol and Old Palm — can range from $3,500 to $10,000 or more per opening. Full-home door replacement projects, which are often the most cost-effective approach from both a protection and permitting standpoint, are priced accordingly based on the specific scope of work.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes these investments accessible through flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down. Our financing programs are designed for homeowners who want to act now rather than wait — because in hurricane country, the cost of doing nothing can far exceed the cost of upgrading. We work with our financing partners to offer terms that fit a variety of budgets, and our team can walk you through your options during a free in-home consultation with no pressure and no obligation.

Beyond the direct cost of installation, the financial case for impact doors in Palm Beach Gardens is strengthened by meaningful homeowner's insurance savings. Many Florida insurance carriers offer significant wind mitigation premium discounts when a home's openings are upgraded to impact-rated products — discounts that can range from 15% to 45% on the wind portion of your policy, which in Palm Beach County is often the largest component of your annual premium. For a home valued at $580,000, those annual savings can reach $1,000 to $2,500 per year, meaning your impact door investment can realistically pay for itself within just a few years through insurance savings alone — before accounting for the protection, security, energy efficiency, and home value appreciation the doors also provide.

section_insurance.h2: Unlock Significant Insurance Savings with Impact Doors in Palm Beach Gardens
section_insurance.body:
Florida homeowner's insurance has become one of the most pressing financial concerns for Palm Beach Gardens residents in recent years. As carriers have exited the state, reduced coverage, or sharply raised premiums, many homeowners in ZIP codes like 33418 and 33410 have seen their annual insurance costs climb dramatically. What many don't realize is that upgrading to impact-rated doors — along with impact windows — is one of the most direct and effective ways to reduce those premiums, often substantially. The Florida wind mitigation credit system, governed by the Office of Insurance Regulation, specifically rewards homeowners who install FBC-compliant impact-rated products on all openings, including doors.

The process begins with a wind mitigation inspection, performed by a licensed inspector who evaluates your home's storm protection features and documents them on a standardized form submitted to your insurance carrier. When all openings — doors and windows — are protected with rated impact products, your home qualifies for the highest tier of opening protection credit, which typically generates the largest available discount on your windstorm premium. In Palm Beach County, where windstorm coverage is often a separate policy component or a large portion of a Citizens Insurance or private carrier policy, this credit can translate to hundreds or even thousands of dollars in annual savings.

Major carriers serving Palm Beach Gardens — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage, and others — all recognize wind mitigation credits and apply them at renewal following an updated inspection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all necessary product approval documentation at the time of installation, making it straightforward for your inspector to document your new impact doors correctly. Homeowners who have upgraded their windows but not yet their doors should note that partial protection typically does not qualify for the maximum credit — a full opening protection upgrade, including all exterior doors, is required to unlock the full benefit. Our team can help you assess what's needed and create a project plan that maximizes both your protection and your insurance savings.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors in Palm Beach Gardens if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Palm Beach Gardens sits in a 170 mph wind design zone under the Florida Building Code, regardless of its distance from the shoreline. Historic storms like Hurricane Frances, Jeanne, and Wilma caused significant damage across the city's inland communities. Wind-borne debris and pressure differentials don't stop at the coastline — and neither do building code requirements. Impact doors protect your home whether you live in PGA National or Old Palm.

faqs.q: How much does it cost to install impact doors in Palm Beach Gardens, and do you offer financing?
faqs.a: Costs vary by door type and configuration, but single entry door installations typically start around $2,500, while sliding glass and French door systems range from $3,500 to $10,000+ per opening. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, making it easy to upgrade now without waiting. We provide free in-home consultations and itemized quotes so there are no surprises.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my doors in Palm Beach Gardens, and does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle that?
faqs.a: Yes — all impact door installations in Palm Beach Gardens require a permit from the City of Palm Beach Gardens Building Division. Permits require product approval documentation and a final inspection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process as a standard part of every project. We pull the permits, prepare the required documentation, and coordinate inspections — so you don't have to navigate the Building Division on your own.

faqs.q: How long does the impact door installation process take in Palm Beach Gardens?
faqs.a: Most single-door replacements can be completed in one day, while larger projects involving multiple openings — such as a full-home door replacement — typically take two to three days. The permitting process through the City of Palm Beach Gardens Building Division generally takes one to three weeks depending on current workload. Our team provides a clear project timeline during your consultation and keeps you informed at every step from permit approval through final inspection.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Palm Beach County?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. Florida's wind mitigation credit system rewards homeowners who upgrade all openings, including doors, with impact-rated products. After installation, a licensed wind mitigation inspector documents your upgrades and submits the findings to your insurer. Palm Beach County homeowners commonly see wind premium reductions of 15% to 45%, which on a $580,000 home can mean annual savings of $1,000 to $2,500 or more. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all product approval documentation needed for your inspection.
